channel.title: Cadence 步频 — PT Research Signal
channel.description: Daily curated physical therapy & rehab research for clinicians. AI-scored signal from 50 sources.

item.title: Effects of physical exercise on spasticity in people with multiple sclerosis: A systematic review.
item.description: This systematic review analyzed the effectiveness of different physical exercise interventions on spasticity in people with multiple sclerosis (EDSS ≤6.0), confirming its positive influence.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: The effectiveness of cardiovascular rehabilitation for transient ischaemic attack and mild stroke: A randomised controlled trial.
item.description: This RCT (n=140) found that a 6-week cardiovascular rehabilitation program improved 6-minute walk test distance and moderate-to-vigorous physical activity at 6 months post-TIA or mild stroke, compared to usual care.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Cross-Education Effect on Force Production Capacity After Unilateral Resistance Training in Clinical Populations: A Systematic Review with Meta-analysis.
item.description: This systematic review with meta-analysis established the cross-education effect after unilateral resistance training (CE-URT) in clinical populations, including those with musculoskeletal conditions and hemiparesis after stroke, demonstrating force production gains in contralateral non-trained muscles.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Cost-Effectiveness of Post-Acute Stroke Rehabilitation From a U.S. Healthcare Payer Perspective: Inpatient Rehabilitation Facilities Versus Skilled Nursing Facilities.
item.description: This decision analytic model evaluated the cost-effectiveness of inpatient rehabilitation facilities (IRFs) versus skilled nursing facilities (SNFs) for stroke survivors in the US, showing IRFs to be more cost-effective from a payer perspective by simulating 90-day outcomes, including mortality and community discharge.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Patient Engagement, Clinical Outcomes, and Satisfaction of an Advanced Orthopedic Virtual Physical Therapy Model: A Retrospective Cohort Study.
item.description: A retrospective cohort study found that an advanced orthopedic virtual physical therapy model achieved 70.9% completion rates—exceeding national benchmarks (43%)—and 96% of completers achieved Minimal Clinically Important Differences in outcomes.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Cognitive Rehabilitation Improves Functional Outcomes in Adults With Post-COVID Cognitive Impairment: Study
item.description: A randomized clinical trial found that cognitive rehabilitation led to significantly greater and sustained improvements in functional goal attainment compared to usual care in adults with post-COVID cognitive impairment.
item.author: Medical Dialogues

item.title: Do blood flow restriction exercises offer additional benefits when compared to conventional exercises in musculoskeletal rehabilitation? A systematic review and meta-analysis.
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis found that low-load exercise with blood flow restriction (LLE-BFR) offers additional benefits over conventional low-load exercise (LLE) and is comparable to high-load exercise (HLE) in improving muscle strength, disability, and pain in individuals with musculoskeletal conditions.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Frontline agreement in diagnosis and treatment for musculoskeletal dysfunctions. A prospective, randomized, controlled study.
item.description: A randomized controlled trial found good agreement in diagnosis, treatment, and discharge decisions between physiotherapists and resident physicians compared with senior physicians in the emergency department for musculoskeletal conditions.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Quantifying Physical Activity Through Step Count Estimation Across Multiple Wearable Sensor Configurations: A Validation Study in Post-Stroke and Healthy Adults.
item.description: A study with 18 post-stroke and 18 healthy adults found that custom peak-detection algorithms, especially with ankle or hip-worn ActiGraph accelerometers, provided superior accuracy for step count estimation compared to manufacturer's algorithms in post-stroke individuals, particularly at lower walking speeds.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Development of a Core Patient-Reported Outcome (Measures) Set for Pediatric Physical Therapy.
item.description: A core set of nine patient-reported outcomes (PROs) grouped into three domains (functioning, general well-being, and social participation) and corresponding valid PROMs has been developed for pediatric physical therapy through focus groups, interviews, a scoping review, and expert panel consensus.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Establishing weighted criteria for return to play after hamstring injury: a systematic review and methodological quality analysis.
item.description: This systematic review identified weighted criteria for return to play after hamstring injury, addressing the high recurrence rate due to lack of consensus.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: GaitEncoder: A Foundation Model of Gait Kinematics for Diverse Clinical Applications and Pathologies
item.description: GaitEncoder is a generative foundation model of walking kinematics for diverse clinical applications and pathologies, enabling actionable insights from smartphone video-based gait analysis using eight aggregated gait datasets.
item.author: medRxiv

item.title: The psychosocial impacts of having eating and drinking difficulties after stroke: a qualitative evidence synthesis.
item.description: A systematic review of 22 studies identified psychological and social impacts as key psychosocial effects of eating and drinking difficulties after stroke, affecting 645 informants.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Sarcopenia Increases Adjacent Segment Degeneration Risk within 3 Years of Anterior Cervical Discectomy and Fusion.
item.description: This retrospective cohort study (n=333) found that sarcopenia significantly increased the risk of radiographic adjacent segment degeneration within 3 years of anterior cervical discectomy and fusion (ACDF).
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Multi-Domain Benefits of Exergaming for Sarcopenia Management in Institutionalized Older Adults: Physical, Psychological, and Social Outcomes.
item.description: This pragmatic quasi-experimental study (n=72) found that a 12-week Nintendo Switch Ring Fit Adventure intervention significantly improved physical, psychological, and social outcomes in institutionalized older adults with sarcopenia.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Use and safety of corticosteroid injections in joints and musculoskeletal soft tissue: guidelines from the American Society of Regional Anesthesia and Pain Medicine, the American Academy of Pain Medicine, the American Society of Interventional Pain Physicians, and the International Pain and Spine Intervention Society
item.description: This provides guidelines from multiple American societies on the use and safety of corticosteroid injections in joints and musculoskeletal soft tissue.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Prevention strategies and modifiable risk factors for upper extremity injury: a systematic review and meta-analysis for the female, woman and girl Athlete Injury pRevention (FAIR) consensus
item.description: A systematic review and meta-analysis for the Female, Woman and Girl Athlete Injury pRevention (FAIR) consensus identified prevention strategies and modifiable risk factors for upper extremity injury.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: Consensus statement on concussion in sport: the 6th International Conference on Concussion in Sport–Amsterdam, October 2022
item.description: This consensus statement from the 6th International Conference on Concussion in Sport provides updated guidelines on concussion management.
item.author: BJSM

item.title: Identifying Gaps in Rehabilitation Strategies for Chronic Stroke: A Systematic Review of Evidence in Patients With and Without Nerve Transfer.
item.description: A systematic review identified gaps in rehabilitation strategies for chronic stroke, highlighting a lack of direct comparisons and detailed rehabilitation protocols between contralateral C7 nerve transfer and technology-assisted/high-dose rehabilitation.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Physical therapy protocols for patients with a megaprosthesis after bone tumor resection.
item.description: This study developed evidence-based physical therapy protocols for patients with megaprostheses after bone tumor resection, covering six different megaprostheses through literature search and expert consensus.
item.author: PubMed
